content.problemStatement:
You are given a permutation PPP of length NNN.

Assume you can perform two types of operations:



Modify PiP_iPi‚Äã (1‚â§i‚â§N)(1 \le i \le N)(1‚â§i‚â§N) to any number at a cost of AAA.




Swap PiP_iPi‚Äã and PjP_jPj‚Äã (1‚â§i,j‚â§N(1 \le i,j \le N(1‚â§i,j‚â§N, i‚âÝj)i \neq j)iÓÄÝ=j) at a cost of BBB.



We define f(P,A,B)f(P, A, B)f(P,A,B) as the minimum cost to make the permutation satisfy Pi=iP_i = iPi‚Äã=i for all iii.

Your task is to calculate:

‚àë1‚â§i,j‚â§Nf(P,i,j)\sum_{1 \leq i, j \leq N} f(P, i, j)1‚â§i,j‚â§N‚àë‚Äãf(P,i,j)Since the answer may be large, you need to output it modulo 998‚Äâ244‚Äâ353998\,244\,353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integers NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PNP_1,P_2,\ldots,P_NP1‚Äã,P2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output ‚àë1‚â§i,j‚â§Nf(P,i,j)\sum_{1 \leq i, j \leq N} f(P, i, j)‚àë1‚â§i,j‚â§N‚Äãf(P,i,j). Since the answer may be large, you need to output it modulo 998‚Äâ244‚Äâ353998\,244\,353998244353.
constraints: It is guaranteed that PPP is a permutation.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.
